content:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding optional on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache/configure_cache

fields.summary: Adding optional on-disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache/configure_cache

content:
type: INTENT
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding compress param (gzip/zlib/None) + magic-header (b'DSPC'+codec byte) encode/decode of pickled bytes in Cache.put/get, plus cull_limit/eviction_policy passed to FanoutCache ctor, and dspy.cache.set_compression() helper
blocked_on: none, but put()/get() bodies will both touch disk_cache read/write - I'll wrap value via self._encode(value)/self._decode(raw) right before disk_cache access so your disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=ttl) call can just become disk_cache.set(key, self._encode(value), expire=ttl) and get() can call self._decode(disk_cache[key]) after your expire() check - let me know if you want to structure differently

fields.summary: Adding compress param (gzip/zlib/None) + magic-header (b'DSPC'+codec byte) encode/decode of pickled bytes in Cache.put/get, plus cull_limit/eviction_policy passed to FanoutCache ctor, and dspy.cache.set_compression() helper
fields.blocked_on: none, but put()/get() bodies will both touch disk_cache read/write - I'll wrap value via self._encode(value)/self._decode(raw) right before disk_cache access so your disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=ttl) call can just become disk_cache.set(key, self._encode(value), expire=ttl) and get() can call self._decode(disk_cache[key]) after your expire() check - let me know if you want to structure differently

content:
type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Compression feature complete: Cache.__init__ now takes compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs, put() calls self._encode_value(value) before disk_cache[key]=..., get() calls self._decode_value(self.disk_cache[key]) - both are plain instance methods so your TTL logic can wrap around them (e.g. disk_cache.set(key, self._encode_value(value), expire=ttl) and self._decode_value(disk_cache.get(key)) with your own expire() check) without touching the encode/decode internals; configure_cache() and DSPY_CACHE now use all-keyword args to Cache() so param ordering won't conflict with your ttl addition

fields.summary: Compression feature complete: Cache.__init__ now takes compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs, put() calls self._encode_value(value) before disk_cache[key]=..., get() calls self._decode_value(self.disk_cache[key]) - both are plain instance methods so your TTL logic can wrap around them (e.g. disk_cache.set(key, self._encode_value(value), expire=ttl) and self._decode_value(disk_cache.get(key)) with your own expire() check) without touching the encode/decode internals; configure_cache() and DSPY_CACHE now use all-keyword args to Cache() so param ordering won't conflict with your ttl addition
